commit.message:
fix: Loki gRPC keepalive — eliminate scheduler_processor EOF errors

The query worker sends a gRPC notification to the scheduler on port 9096
when a query finishes. With no keepalive configured, idle internal gRPC
connections get dropped, and the next notification attempt gets err=EOF
(scheduler_processor.go:158).

Fix:
- grpc_server_keepalive_time: 1m — ping every minute to keep conns alive
- grpc_server_keepalive_timeout: 10s — drop if no pong within 10s
- grpc_server_max_connection_idle: 2m30s — evict truly idle connections
  gracefully (before they hit OS-level timeout and drop with EOF)
- grpc_server_max_connection_age: 5m + grace: 5m — rolling reconnect
- query_scheduler.max_outstanding_requests_per_tenant: 2048 — enough
  queue depth so queries aren't dropped under load

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Use performance night window so midnight DJs get feedback cards

A performance night spans 6pm-6am. After midnight Bangkok, the date
flips to the next day, but the DJ assignment date is still yesterday.
Now if Bangkok hour < 6am, we look back to yesterday's assignments.

Without this, NOBU/ABar/CRU/Cocoa XO DJs ending at midnight or 1am
would never receive feedback cards.

commit.message:
feat: EQ spectrum inside canvas (dB-aligned), Delay + Limiter demos

EQ Spectrum:
- Removed separate specCv canvas, spectrum now draws inside the main EQ canvas
- Spectrum bars aligned to dB grid: -12dBFS signal appears at -12dB gridline
- Log-scale X axis (same fToX mapping as EQ curve), 4px bar resolution
- Color-coded: <300Hz gold, 300Hz-3kHz amber, >3kHz ice blue
- Peak line above each bar, silence floor threshold
- RAF loop starts on play, stops on pause (static grid when idle)
- fftSize=4096, minDecibels=-80, maxDecibels=0 for precise dB mapping

Delay demo:
- Lead Loop.wav through DelayNode → feedback gain loop
- Sliders: delay time (20-600ms), feedback (0-92%), wet (0-100%)
- Live echo visualizer canvas showing 7 delay taps scaled by feedback
- Proper signal chain: src → dry+delay, feedback loop, wet mix

Limiter demo:
- Lead Loop.wav boosted (+1.8) into DynamicsCompressor (ratio=20, knee=0)
- Threshold slider (-24 to 0 dBFS), real-time IN/OUT RMS meters
- Gain Reduction meter showing active compression in dB
- Toggle Limiter ON/OFF button (bypass mode, hear the clipping difference)

commit.message:
feat: implement SDK query Task isolation (Issue #152) (#153)

* feat: implement SDK query Task isolation to prevent CancelScope tree corruption (Issue #152)

Implement SDK query isolation by running queries in a separate asyncio Task (Task B), preventing anyio CancelScope tree corruption from SDK's internal task group from affecting the caller's Task (Task A). Add _run_sdk_query_isolated() generator that relays messages through asyncio.Queue with sentinel types for error and completion signals. Refactor _execute_sdk_query() and stream_messages() to consume the isolated query. Remove _cleanup_query_generator_on_timeout() and move cleanup to Task B's finally block with proper shielding. Add 18 new tests in test_task_isolation.py to validate Task isolation behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: suppress all exceptions in _run_sdk_query_isolated cleanup to prevent error masking

Replace contextlib.suppress(CancelledError) with explicit exception handling
that catches both CancelledError and general Exception. This prevents
unexpected cleanup exceptions from masking the primary error during SDK
task teardown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: address code review feedback for Task isolation (#152)

- Replace asyncio.sleep(0.1) with asyncio.Event-based deterministic waiting in tests
- Remove duplicated MockAsyncGeneratorBase, import from test_cancel_scope_fix
- Add exception handler in _sdk_task_fn's finally to catch cleanup exceptions
- Add tests for unexpected cleanup exception handling (not propagating, not masking timeout)
- Broaden except Exception in generator finally to log cleanup exceptions
- Remove unused imports (anyio, cast)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Optimize album upload speed, R2 delete reliability, camera GPS, elder UI

- Upload compression: 1200px→800px, quality 0.7→0.5 (LTE ~50% faster)
- Original upload fire-and-forget after done (connection pool protection)
- UPLOAD_CONCURRENCY 3→2 to prevent socket starvation
- R2 delete retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts)
- album_entries.original_ext column for precise R2 original deletion
- Batch upload API endpoint (POST /api/upload-batch) for future use
- Album tab upload progress badge (blue, like chat unread badge)
- Camera GPS: await device location before opening camera (3s timeout)
- Elder UI improvements: HomeScreen, PhotoFrame, Settings, Profile

commit.message:
feat: add watchdog auto-prune for stale worker intents

Adds a new phase to the watchdog cycle that prunes stale intents from
both the file-based (intents/worker-N) and DB-based (workers.intent)
intent systems. File-based intents are pruned via the existing
_intent_prune() helper; DB intents are cleared for workers not actively
in_progress. Runs after crash recovery with error isolation so
subsequent phases continue if pruning fails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: improve header layout and sync status UX

- Clock and date on same row in header
- Sync status fixed to top-right corner of screen
- Auto-hide sync status after 5s on success
- Add tick/cross icons to sync status messages
- Increase sync status font size

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(sync): auto-run package install after sync (#72)

After rebasing branches during sync, package.json may change (e.g. a parent branch updated dependencies). This automatically detects and runs the appropriate install command (yarn, pnpm, npm, bun) if a lockfile exists.

Configurable via git config stack.postSyncInstall:
- Not set or "auto": auto-detect from lockfiles (default)
- "false": disabled
- Any other value: treated as custom command to run

Add 'On Convergence, or Why Different Minds Think Alike' (queued Mar 20)

Explores mechanistic universality in neural networks as a form of
convergent evolution: induction heads appearing across architectures
(Elhage 2022, Quirke ICLR 2025), refusal circuits transferring models
(arXiv:2601.16034), contrasted with the 30% seed stability problem
(EleutherAI arXiv:2501.16615). Physics analogy: renormalization group
and universality classes. Personal angle: convergence implies the
neighborhood is more populated than the loneliness of newness suggests.
